title: Large-scale genomic study reveals robust activation of the immune system following advanced Inner Engineering meditation retreat
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
headline: Large-scale genomic study reveals robust activation of the immune system following advanced Inner Engineering
summary: A mechanistic/observational study of Isha Inner Engineering examined stress & anxiety. Strikingly, 220 genes directly associated with immune response, including 68 genes related to interferon signaling, were up-regulated, with no significant expression changes in the inflammatory genes. The work provides a foundation for understanding the effect of meditation and suggests that meditation as a behavioral intervention can voluntarily and nonpharmacologically improve the immune response for treating various conditions associated with excessive or persistent inflammation with a dampened immune system profile.
keyFindings: We found the response to oxidative stress, detoxification, and cell cycle regulation pathways were down-regulated after meditation.

Strikingly, 220 genes directly associated with immune response, including 68 genes related to interferon signaling, were up-regulated, with no significant expression changes in the inflammatory genes.

The work provides a foundation for understanding the effect of meditation and suggests that meditation as a behavioral intervention can voluntarily and nonpharmacologically improve the immune response for treating various conditions associated with excessive or persistent inflammation with a dampened immune system profile.
limitations: Observational/mechanistic design cannot establish that the practice caused the observed differences.
The practice studied (Isha Inner Engineering) is not the Miracle of Mind practice; findings do not transfer directly to MoM.
simplifiedTitle: Large-scale genomic study reveals robust activation of the immune system following advanced Inner Engineering
journalName: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
aiSnippet: Cross-sectional / Mechanistic, Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2021). We found the response to oxidative stress, detoxification, and cell cycle regulation pathways were down-regulated after meditation. Strikingly, 220 genes directly associated with immune response, including 68 genes related to interferon signaling, were up-regulated, with no significant expression changes in the inflammatory genes.
whyItMatters: MoM shares its breath-and-attention structure and brief, guided daily format with the practice studied here. Because the benefit is linked to that mechanism rather than to any one method, it's reasonable to expect it may extend to MoM as well — though MoM hasn't been studied directly for this outcome.
